title: euxolus lineatus
intro: Euxolus lineatus, also known as the Indian snakeroot or Rauwolfia, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and snakebites, due to its alkaloid content, including reserpine and yohimbine. The plant has been found to have health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, and has been used as an antipsychotic and antihypertensive agent. In traditional herbal preparations, Euxolus lineatus is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or ointments, and has been used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for centuries.
